parts.text:
Create a horizontal bar chart with a dark background football analytics style.
    Data (season — player (club) — big chances created):
    2010/11 — Didier Drogba (Chelsea) — 21
2011/12 — Robin van Persie (Arsenal) — 21
2012/13 — Leighton Baines (Everton) — 21
2013/14 — Luis Suárez (Liverpool) — 22
2014/15 — Cesc Fàbregas (Chelsea) — 16
2015/16 — Mesut Özil (Arsenal) — 29
2016/17 — Kevin De Bruyne (Man City) — 24
2017/18 — Kevin De Bruyne (Man City) — 19
2018/19 — Ryan Fraser (Bournemouth) — 28
2019/20 — Kevin De Bruyne (Man City) — 33
2020/21 — Bruno Fernandes (Man United) — 20
2021/22 — Harry Kane (Tottenham) — 19
2022/23 — Bruno Fernandes (Man United) — 32
2023/24 — Mohamed Salah (Liverpool) — 22
2024/25 — Mohamed Salah (Liverpool) — 27
2025/26 — Bruno Fernandes (Man United) — 24
    Seasons on Y-axis (oldest at bottom), big chances created on X-axis.
    Color each bar by player — same player always gets the same color across seasons.
    Label each bar with player name and club. Annotate De Bruyne 2019/20 (33) as the all-time record.
    Title: 'Premier League — Big Chances Created Leader by Season (2010/11–2025/26)'.
    Use a sleek dark background (#0d1117), neon green or cyan accent for the record bar, muted tones for others.
    Include a subtle pitch-green bottom border or watermark stripe. No gridlines, minimal clutter.

Create a horizontal bar chart in a classic newspaper editorial infographic style.
    Data (season — player (club) — big chances created):
    2010/11 — Didier Drogba (Chelsea) — 21
2011/12 — Robin van Persie (Arsenal) — 21
2012/13 — Leighton Baines (Everton) — 21
2013/14 — Luis Suárez (Liverpool) — 22
2014/15 — Cesc Fàbregas (Chelsea) — 16
2015/16 — Mesut Özil (Arsenal) — 29
2016/17 — Kevin De Bruyne (Man City) — 24
2017/18 — Kevin De Bruyne (Man City) — 19
2018/19 — Ryan Fraser (Bournemouth) — 28
2019/20 — Kevin De Bruyne (Man City) — 33
2020/21 — Bruno Fernandes (Man United) — 20
2021/22 — Harry Kane (Tottenham) — 19
2022/23 — Bruno Fernandes (Man United) — 32
2023/24 — Mohamed Salah (Liverpool) — 22
2024/25 — Mohamed Salah (Liverpool) — 27
2025/26 — Bruno Fernandes (Man United) — 24
    Seasons on Y-axis (oldest at bottom), big chances created on X-axis.
    Use a cream/off-white background with serif fonts (like a newspaper).
    Color bars in muted ink tones — one color per unique player.
    Add a bold headline at the top: 'Who Creates the Most Big Chances?'
    Subtitle: 'Premier League season leaders, 2010/11–2025/26'.
    Add thin horizontal dividers between every 2 seasons for readability.
    Annotate De Bruyne 2019/20 with a small star icon and 'All-time record' label.

Create a clean minimalist horizontal bar chart on a pure white background.
    Data (season — player (club) — big chances created):
    2010/11 — Didier Drogba (Chelsea) — 21
2011/12 — Robin van Persie (Arsenal) — 21
2012/13 — Leighton Baines (Everton) — 21
2013/14 — Luis Suárez (Liverpool) — 22
2014/15 — Cesc Fàbregas (Chelsea) — 16
2015/16 — Mesut Özil (Arsenal) — 29
2016/17 — Kevin De Bruyne (Man City) — 24
2017/18 — Kevin De Bruyne (Man City) — 19
2018/19 — Ryan Fraser (Bournemouth) — 28
2019/20 — Kevin De Bruyne (Man City) — 33
2020/21 — Bruno Fernandes (Man United) — 20
2021/22 — Harry Kane (Tottenham) — 19
2022/23 — Bruno Fernandes (Man United) — 32
2023/24 — Mohamed Salah (Liverpool) — 22
2024/25 — Mohamed Salah (Liverpool) — 27
2025/26 — Bruno Fernandes (Man United) — 24
    Seasons on Y-axis (oldest at bottom), big chances created on X-axis.
    Use a single accent color (coral or electric blue) for all bars.
    Highlight De Bruyne 2019/20 in a contrasting dark color with a subtle arrow annotation.
    No borders, no background fill on bars, ultra-thin bar height with generous white space between rows.
    Label only player name (no club) at the end of each bar. Sans-serif font throughout.
    Title in small caps at the top left: 'Big Chances Created — PL Leaders by Season'.

Create a square social media card (1080x1080) horizontal bar chart optimized for Instagram or X posting.
    Data (season — player (club) — big chances created):
    2010/11 — Didier Drogba (Chelsea) — 21
2011/12 — Robin van Persie (Arsenal) — 21
2012/13 — Leighton Baines (Everton) — 21
2013/14 — Luis Suárez (Liverpool) — 22
2014/15 — Cesc Fàbregas (Chelsea) — 16
2015/16 — Mesut Özil (Arsenal) — 29
2016/17 — Kevin De Bruyne (Man City) — 24
2017/18 — Kevin De Bruyne (Man City) — 19
2018/19 — Ryan Fraser (Bournemouth) — 28
2019/20 — Kevin De Bruyne (Man City) — 33
2020/21 — Bruno Fernandes (Man United) — 20
2021/22 — Harry Kane (Tottenham) — 19
2022/23 — Bruno Fernandes (Man United) — 32
2023/24 — Mohamed Salah (Liverpool) — 22
2024/25 — Mohamed Salah (Liverpool) — 27
2025/26 — Bruno Fernandes (Man United) — 24
    Dark gradient background (deep navy to black). Bold rounded bars colored by player.
    Large bold title at top: '🏴󠁧󠁢󠁥󠁮󠁧󠁿 Big Chances Created — PL Leaders'.
    Small subtitle: 'One winner per season, 2010/11–2025/26'.
    Player name + value displayed inside or at end of each bar in white text.
    De Bruyne 2019/20 bar pulsing gold with a 👑 emoji and 'RECORD' badge.
    Add a small footer: 'Data via StatsBomb | @YourHandle'.
    High contrast, bold, shareable aesthetic.

Create a horizontal bar chart in a retro vintage football poster style.
    Data (season — player (club) — big chances created):
    2010/11 — Didier Drogba (Chelsea) — 21
2011/12 — Robin van Persie (Arsenal) — 21
2012/13 — Leighton Baines (Everton) — 21
2013/14 — Luis Suárez (Liverpool) — 22
2014/15 — Cesc Fàbregas (Chelsea) — 16
2015/16 — Mesut Özil (Arsenal) — 29
2016/17 — Kevin De Bruyne (Man City) — 24
2017/18 — Kevin De Bruyne (Man City) — 19
2018/19 — Ryan Fraser (Bournemouth) — 28
2019/20 — Kevin De Bruyne (Man City) — 33
2020/21 — Bruno Fernandes (Man United) — 20
2021/22 — Harry Kane (Tottenham) — 19
2022/23 — Bruno Fernandes (Man United) — 32
2023/24 — Mohamed Salah (Liverpool) — 22
2024/25 — Mohamed Salah (Liverpool) — 27
2025/26 — Bruno Fernandes (Man United) — 24
    Seasons on Y-axis (oldest at bottom), big chances created on X-axis.
    Use a worn parchment or aged yellowed background texture.
    Bars in bold retro color palette: brick red, forest green, navy, mustard yellow — one per player.
    Serif or slab-serif fonts throughout. Title styled like a 1970s football programme cover:
    'THE PREMIER LEAGUE BIG CHANCE CREATORS — A Season-by-Season Record'.
    Add decorative dividers between eras (e.g. 2010s vs 2020s).
    Annotate De Bruyne 2019/20 with a vintage badge icon and 'Record Holder' ribbon.
    Distressed edges and slight grain texture overlay for authenticity.
